title: U.S. Is Said to Be Investigating George Santos Over Kalshi Betting
description: The former congressman was referred to federal authorities after he bet on his own attendance at the State of the Union address, a person familiar with the matter said.
author: David Yaffe-Bellany and Sharon LaFraniere

title: Trump Administration to Dismantle the Ocean Observatories Initiative
description: The $368 million network of instruments collecting data in both the Atlantic and Pacific has been critical to climate and ocean research.
author: Eric Niiler

title: CBS News Fires Scott Pelley of ‘60 Minutes’
description: Mr. Pelley, a former “CBS Evening News” anchor, was ousted after months of tensions between staff and Bari Weiss, the network’s editor in chief.
author: Benjamin Mullin and Michael M. Grynbaum

title: America Broke Something When It Gave Trump a Second Chance
description: Lincoln would know how to deal with these Republicans.
author: Jamelle Bouie

title: Trump Has Failed as Commander in Chief
description: Trump has failed to unite the country while at war and instead is seeking personal gain.
author: Thomas L. Friedman

title: Supreme Court Clears the Way for Republican-Friendly Map in Alabama
description: In the first major case since the justices narrowed the Voting Rights Act, the court cleared the way for Alabama to use a map preferred by Republicans that leaves the state with only one majority-Black district.
author: Abbie VanSickle and Emily Cochrane

title: Trump Signs Executive Order Seeking Oversight of A.I. Models
description: The order, which signaled a shift from the hands-off approach the White House had previously taken toward A.I., followed debates over how to gain control of A.I. models without disrupting innovation.
author: Sheera Frenkel and Tripp Mickle

title: Peabo Bryson, Soul Singer Known as the Voice of Love, Dies at 75
description: He was admired for impassioned duets with singers like Roberta Flack and Celine Dion and for the Disney hits “Beauty and the Beast” and “A Whole New World.”
author: Clay Risen

title: Protests Grow in Albania Over Kushner-Linked Project
description: Plans for luxury resorts on the Adriatic Sea have long worried environmentalists. The developments are now a flashpoint for broader frustrations with the government.
author: Amelia Nierenberg

title: Trump Endorses Tom Kean Jr., the N.J. Congressman Who Hasn’t Been Seen For Months
description: The message from President Trump about Representative Thomas Kean Jr. comes as voters head to the polls on Tuesday. Mr. Kean’s name is on the ballot, but he hasn’t been seen for months.
author: Tracey Tully

title: How the Oprah Tax Code Swept America
description: Here’s how to make taxes work.
author: Natasha Sarin

title: Marilyn Monroe Fans Descended on Palm Springs For Her 100th Birthday
description: To honor her centennial, over 1,000 Marilyn Monroe fans came together to dress as the screen legend in Palm Springs, where her Hollywood career began.
author: Francesca Forquet

title: Minnesota Republicans Hold Moment of Silence for Derek Chauvin
description: Delegates to the Minnesota Republican Party’s convention voted to hold a moment of silence for Derek Chauvin, who was convicted of murdering George Floyd.
author: Mitch Smith and Julie Bosman

title: What Ireland and Germany Can Teach Us About Birthright Citizenship
description: President Trump has argued that the United States is “stupid” for granting citizenship at birth. Most countries don’t do so, but that can create problems.
author: Lydia DePillis, Megan Specia and Tatiana Firsova

title: Trump Backs Off Plan for $1.8 Billion Fund That Drew Political Backlash
description: The fund has drawn backlash from critics who said it was a scheme to reward President Trump’s political allies with public benefits.
author: Tyler Pager, Maggie Haberman, Alan Feuer, Devlin Barrett and Annie Karni

title: The New Zealand Parakeet Pair That Are Saving Their Species
description: There are about 450 orange-fronted parakeets left in the world. Super breeders Nacho and Trixie are doing their best to bolster the population.
author: Laura Chung

title: Trump Administration Announces Stricter Rules for Medicaid Work Requirement
description: By tightening an exception for people with serious illnesses, more of them may lose coverage.
author: Margot Sanger-Katz and Sarah Kliff

title: Hegseth Strikes Female and Black Navy Officers From Promotion List
description: The defense secretary’s decision to block the officers’ promotions appears driven by his anti-diversity stance rather than based on merit.
author: Greg Jaffe and Kate Kelly

title: Demonstrators Cannot Be Forced to Take Down ‘86-47’ Flag, Judge Rules
description: A federal judge wrote that the phrase, which also led to charges against the former F.B.I. director, James Comey, did not appear to constitute a true threat.
author: Zach Montague

title: California’s Public Universities Went All in on A.I. Now They’re Tearing Themselves Apart.
description: California’s public universities spent $16.9 million on A.I. during a financial crisis, and the result has been chaos.
author: Linda Kinstler
